items.title: US-Iran peace talks to begin, after Tehran closed Strait of Hormuz
items.description: Ahead of negotiations in Switzerland, The Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps has said vessels should not approach the strait of Hormuz else their security would be at risk. Iran shut the shipping lane on Saturday, saying that Israeli strikes in Lebanon constituted a breach of the preliminary ceasefire, agreed between the US and Israel on Wednesday. Iranian and American delegations are set to meet in Switzerland on Sunday to work out their interim agreement to end the war. Story by Caroline Baum and Camille Corcoran.

items.title: Iran claims Lebanon 'main topic' at peace talks with US
items.description: The United States and Iran are set to start a new round of talks in Switzerland on Sunday in a bid to finalise the Memorandum of Understanding signed last week. The teams have a timeframe of 60 days to resolve some of the more difficult issues such as what will happen to Iran's nuclear program and stockpiles of uranium. However the talks have already been shrouded in uncertainty as Israel and Hezbollah - not parties to the agreement - have continued to clash in Lebanon, with Tel Aviv refusing to withdraw from the territory. Story by Eliza Herbert and details by FRANCE 24 correspondent in Geneva, John Zarocostas.

Emergency services and military forces were also put on wildfire alert as about a third of France was placed under the national weather service’s heat red alert with temperatures expected to reach 40 Celsius.

items.title: Spain PM's wife Begoña Gomez to stand trial for corruption
items.description: On Saturday, a Spanish judge ordered Begoña Gomez, wife of Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez, to stand trial for corruption. She is accused of using her position as the PM's wife for personal gain by securing work contracts. Gomez is now banned from leaving the country and must report to court twice a month until a verdict has been reached. This trial is the latest in a series of corruption inquiries surrounding, but not directly naming, Sanchez. The growing scandals risk toppling his minority ruling coalition. Story by Caroline Baum.

items.title: Moscow residents complain of black rain after largest Ukrainian attack hits oil refinery
items.description: A refinery and a shopping centre burned after almost 200 Ukrainian drones were launched towards an area to the south-east of the Russian capital.

items.title: Minority Caucus Appoints Afam Ogene as Spokesperson
items.description: The caucus announced that Afam Ogene, a senior lawmaker and former journalist, will serve as its spokesperson and help promote accountability, transparency, and democratic governance. The post Minority Caucus Appoints Afam Ogene as Spokesperson appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: What Iran and US get from deal and why both could struggle to keep it
items.description: BBC analysts assess the claims by both Washington and Tehran to have won a victory with the deal to end their war.

items.title: Bolivia’s President declares emergency over blockade crisis, paving way to deploy military
items.description: The move came in a ‌live message to the ‌nation ⁠just hours after Bolivian President Paz unveiled ⁠a deal struck with the main union, the Bolivian Workers’ Confederation, that ​aimed to ease tension

items.title: Trump threatens to charge U.S. tolls in Strait of Hormuz if final Iran deal not reached in 60 days
items.description: The announcements indicated a rough start to technical-level U.S.-Iran talks that key mediator Pakistan said will begin Sunday (June 21, 2026), with Qatari mediators also participating

items.title: Israeli envoy and UN official clash at hearing on children in conflict
items.description: At a ⁠meeting in New York to mark the International Day for the Elimination of Sexual Violence in Conflict, the ‌Israeli envoy, Danny Danon, demanded the resignation of Pramila Patten, who produced a report that ‌blacklisted Israel for such alleged abuses for ‌the ⁠first time, accusing her of bias.

items.title: Macron and Modi tour Indian pavilion at France's VivaTech
items.description: France highlighted its technology partnership with India on 18 June as President Emmanuel Macron and Prime Minister Narendra Modi toured VivaTech 2026 in Paris.

items.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency
items.description: The move comes after weeks of anti-government protests that have caused a shortage of basic goods in Bolivia.

items.title: Counterterror police investigate after five hurt in Edinburgh attacks appeared to target Muslims
items.description: Police Scotland said that a 36-year-old man was arrested late on Friday (June 19, 2026) after officers received multiple reports of attacks in the west and north of the city

items.title: Bangladesh PM to visit Malaysia, China on first foreign tour
items.description: Bangladesh's Prime Minister Tarique Rahman will visit Malaysia on Sunday (June 21) before travelling to China the following day, Dhaka's state-run BSS news agency reported, citing Foreign Ministry officials.

items.title: Live: James Tedesco in the wars as Roosters try to hold off Sharks
items.description: Referee Ashley Klein asks Roosters captain James Tedesco to clean some of the blood off his face before speaking to him during the second half of the Roosters' clash with the Sharks. Follow live.

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Catholic Church urges Congress to reject Milei's property bill
items.description: Church bodies warn that Milei's government's 'Inviolability of Private Property' bill threatens national sovereignty over land, water and natural resources and call on lawmakers to vote it down. Leer más

items.title: Russia, Madagascar foreign ministers hold talks in Moscow
items.description: Russia's Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov welcomed his Madagascar counterpart, Alice N'Diaye, in Moscow on Friday

items.title: Iran war day 114: US, Iranian delegations in Switzerland for key talks
items.description: Lebanon to top the agenda as US and Iran to hold talks in Switzerland’s Burgenstock mediated by Qatar and Pakistan.

items.title: Do Middle East nations still see the U.S. as a reliable ally?
items.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Dania Thafer of the Gulf International Forum whether countries in the Middle East consider the U.S. a reliable ally.

items.title: Celebrations as Mexico becomes first nation to reach World Cup knockout stage
items.description: Thousands of jubilant Mexico supporters celebrated across the country on 18 June after their team became the first nation to secure a place in the World Cup knockout stage with a 1-0 victory over South Korea.

items.title: Six-year-old Ebola patient taken from DR Congo hospital found and 'doing well'
items.description: Health facilities have come under attack during the current outbreak as a result of misinformation and fear.

items.description: Cabinet chief’s documentation attempts to explain an astronomical rise in his net worth. His legal strategy and mounting complications in Congress. Leer más

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: Undav heroics help Germany beat Ivory Coast
items.description: In Germany's second 2026 World Cup game, Deniz Undav helped Germany to a comeback win against Ivory Coast in Toronto.

items.title: Lebanon state media says five dead in fresh Israeli strikes on south
items.description: The state-run National News Agency (NNA) reported Israeli airstrikes on more than a dozen south Lebanon locations after midnight and into Saturday, June 20 morning

items.title: US, Iran sign 'memorandum of understanding' to end Middle East war
items.description: US President Donald Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian sign a memorandum of understanding, ending regional war that erupted on February 28 with US-Israeli strikes. Leer más

items.title: South Africa ties Czech Republic 1-1, Mexico goes to knockout stage
items.description: South Africa keeps its World Cup hopes alive with a 1-1 draw against the Czech Republic, while Mexico reached the knockout stage after a 1-0 win against South Africa on Thursday.

items.title: Trump escalates spat over photo at G7 Summit; Meloni hits back at ‘constant, senseless attacks’
items.description: The President hit out at the Italian PM, alleging that she “wants to be friends again” after U.S. “defeated” Iran militarily

items.title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz shut as U.S.-Iran talks set for Sunday in Switzerland
items.description: U.S. and Iranian teams are set to hold "technical-level" talks, according to Pakistan's foreign ministry. But fighting in Lebanon and claims over the Strait of Hormuz threaten a tentative agreement.

items.title: US and Iran face make-or-break talks in Switzerland
items.description: Iranian and US officials are set for high stakes talks in Switzerland to reinforce the Memorandum of Understanding.

items.title: Malaysia’s 3rd International Summit of Religious Leaders explores how faith can empower the youth
items.description: Religious leaders, scholars and students gathered in Kuala Lumpur for the Third International Summit of Religious Leaders, focusing on empowering youth in an era of rapid technological and social change.

items.title: Argentina MSCI review holds key to foreign investment surge
items.description: MSCI Inc will decide next week whether to set Argentina on the road back to membership in global stock indexes. Leer más

items.title: Israel says it's cutting ties to EU's Kaja Kallas
items.description: Israel's foreign minister says he is severing contact with the EU's foreign affairs chief Kaja Kallas. Gideon Saar attributed the move to a media report alleging that Kallas compared Israel to apartheid-era South Africa.

items.title: US curbs Anthropic AI access, raising global concerns
items.description: The Trump administration has restricted foreign access to Anthropic's latest AI models over security fears, forcing a global shutdown. Could the row hurt the Claude-maker's IPO plans and set a far-reaching precedent?

items.title: How cities are adapting to a warming world
items.description: As heat waves grow more frequent and fierce, cities worldwide are racing to cool streets and reshape urban life.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Brobbey, Gakpo score braces as rampant Netherlands crush Sweden
items.description: Brian Brobbey and Cody Gakpo scored two goals each as the Netherlands thumped Sweden 5-1 in Houston on Saturday to move top of Group F and put one foot in the World Cup knockouts.

items.title: Brazil's Lula signs decree to freeze illegal betting platform funds
UK actress charged with importing meth worth almost A$300m into Australia
items.description: Emaa Hussen, 34, faces life in prison for allegedly trying to smuggle 320kg of meth hidden in bags of charcoal.

items.title: Haitians hope for a victory against Brazil in the World Cup
items.description: Haitians are fervent soccer fans and deeply patriotic toward their nation — the world’s first Black-led republic and the second independent republic in the Americas after the U.S. But for decades, they’ve rooted for Brazil.

items.title: Iran-US talks called off amid renewed Lebanon fighting, leaving next steps unclear
items.description: A formal signing ceremony and talks planned for Friday in Switzerland between Iran and the United States were called off, as fighting between Israel and Hezbollah intensified in southern Lebanon.

items.title: In Trump's shadow, Vance becomes face of Iran deal
items.description: His fierce defence of the Iran plan amid mounting criticism comes as speculation intensifies about a possible 2028 presidential run.

items.title: Trial into disappearance of missing five-year-old Loan begins
items.description: Fate of five-year-old Loan Danilo Peña remains one of Argentina’s most haunting mysteries of recent years; Long-awaited trial due to begin, days after the second anniversary of child’s disappearance. Leer más

items.title: Speedway plans spark protest
items.description: Residents of Beachmere in Moreton Bay say a proposed speedway track will “fundamentally change the character" of the small coastal town.

items.title: 10 years after Brexit, could the UK rejoin the European Union?
items.description: It has been nearly a decade since Brexit and calls for the United Kingdom to rejoin the bloc are growing - particularly amongst progressives. The issue has been further spotlighted amid the current political uncertainty, with Prime Minister Keir Starmer's job on the line, facing a leadership challenge from Andy Burnham. But will a change in government lead to reaccession? Answer with Eliza Herbert.

items.title: Zimbabwe MPs pass bill to extend president's time in power
items.description: The proposal would extend Emmerson Mnangagwa's term by two years and scrap direct presidential elections.

items.title: What you need to know about the preliminary U.S.-Iran agreement signed by Trump
items.description: Here's a look at the preliminary agreement between the U.S. and Iran, and the challenges that remain to find lasting peace.

items.title: France bans alcohol at music festival events under red heatwave alert
items.description: Annual street parties attended by millions ordered to help “preserve” healthcare services in 40C (104F) heat.

items.title: Can Europe build its own AI heavyweights to rival the US?
items.description: With AI dominated by US firms, Europe is pushing for more independence. New partnerships, investments and startups aim to boost the continent's digital sovereignty, but challenges around funding and regulation remain.

items.title: Nathaniel Brown: The left back Germany have been waiting for
